title: EVICTION DEFENSE PROJECT - 211info
text: OREGON LAW CENTER STATEWIDE EVICTION DEFENSE PROJECT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal assistance and representation to tenants facing eviction court cases. Must contact agency before first court appearance date. Eligibility: Low-income households who have an eviction or vacate notice, and are at 125% Federal Poverty Level (FPL) or less (some exceptions for those slightly above, on a case-by case basis) Hours: Monday-Wednesday 9am-1pm Email (888) 585-9638 Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (800) 672-4919 Intake Intake (888) 585-9638 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Visit website or call for information. If leaving a voice message, include name, case number, and court summons date. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, or interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Baker, Benton, Clackamas, Clatsop, Columbia, Coos, Crook, Curry, Deschutes, Douglas, Gilliam, Grant, Harney, Hood River, Jackson, Jefferson, Josephine, Klamath, Lake, Lane, Lincoln, Linn, Malheur, Morrow, Polk, Sherman, Tillamook, Umatilla, Union, Wallowa, Wasco, Washington, Wheeler, and Yamhill Counties Services: Eviction Prevention Assistance If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. No Mapping Information Available
